comments.selectedText: In Two Minutes
comments.comment: clarify that the roll changes in combat, more. Boons are universal and apply both in combat and in a normal roll. clarity here
comments.resolution: Rewrote the combat paragraph to lead with 'the core roll changes' and clarified weapon dice replace stat dice for attacks. Added a sentence after the stakes paragraph explaining Boons/Snags are universal modifiers that work the same on every roll.

comments.selectedText: Roll d6s equal to the relevant stat. Read the single highest die.
comments.comment: here as well, pay dice for gambits
comments.resolution: Added Gambit explanation directly after the bold roll instruction: players may sacrifice dice before reading results to pay for Gambits (tactical side effects), with examples like pocketing a key or slamming a door shut.

comments.selectedText: When you do something risky, roll d6s equal to your stat. Read the highest die.
comments.comment: Verify immediate highlight after save
comments.resolution: Test comment - cleaned up

comments.selectedText: Guard keeps you on your feet. When it breaks, real injuries start.
comments.comment: Test multi-node highlight (crosses bold boundary)
comments.resolution: Test comment - cleaned up

comments.selectedText: C&S paycheck.
comments.comment: remove this, secret for now
comments.resolution: Removed 'C&S paycheck.' from Jack's background line. Now reads: 'Schwartz-Zee security lead. Sumerian Knot veteran.'

comments.selectedText: Looks like the service pistol. Doesn’t feel like it.
comments.comment: change this to a Knot personal equipment
comments.resolution: Renamed to 'Knot sidearm' — Sumerian Knot issue, sand-scored grip, contact plate retrofitted onto trigger guard. Tied to Jack's military past.
comments.resolvedText: Knot sidearm (1d6, Sidearm). Sumerian Knot issue. Sand-scored grip, contact plate retrofitted onto the trigger guard. Came back with you.

comments.selectedText: tructural Intuition. Boon on rolls to assess structural integrity, find weak points, or navigate enclosed spaces. You read rock and steel the way most people read faces.
comments.comment: this seems out of place, something else that fits the character?
comments.resolution: Replaced with 'Creature Sign' — Boon on Sharp rolls to read tracks, droppings, territorial marks, disturbed ground. Fits Brenor's field naturalist identity.
comments.resolvedText: Creature Sign. Boon on Sharp rolls to read tracks, droppings, territorial marks, or disturbed ground.

comments.selectedText:
Geological probe. Handheld contact-plate rod. Reads material composition and structural stress.

    Stress Fracture (1 Guard): Identifies the exact point where a structure will fail. Boon on the next roll to break, collapse, or exploit that weak point.
comments.comment: again a bit off, fit more to the character, maybe something to fend of wildlife
comments.resolution: Replaced with 'Thauma-flare launcher' — field-researcher wildlife deterrent. Concussive Burst (1 Guard) fires a thaumic flash-bang that forces creatures to flee.
comments.resolvedText: Thauma-flare launcher. Contact plate on the breech. Standard field-researcher deterrent for leyline-agitated fauna.

comments.selectedText: Sharp: Awareness, reflexes, precision, instinct. Spotting danger, dodging a swing, picking a lock, reading a room.
comments.comment: something about intelligence, math, calculation
comments.resolution: Sharp now reads 'awareness, reflexes, precision, analytical thinking' with examples 'working through a problem, connecting dots.' Resolved in previous session.

comments.selectedText: a Snag lasts one action instead of a turn.
comments.comment: what is a turn here? we should talk n turns, a scene or until a clear condition maybe?
comments.resolution: Changed 'one action instead of a full round' to 'one action instead of until end of next turn'. Stunned condition also updated to use 'end of your next action'. Consistent action-based duration language throughout.

comments.selectedText: project a ward, activate a scanner, trigger a siphon)
comments.comment: these examples are neboulus, do not get it really
comments.resolution: Rewrote device gambit examples with concrete effects: deadfield rod kills all thaumatech in the room, BTC scanner locks onto a shaper through walls, ward projector throws a barrier across a doorway. Added ‘These aren’t rolls. They just work.’

comments.selectedText: usually 1 round
comments.comment: again since you have one action per round, round = action, what do we do here?
comments.resolution: Stunned now reads 'until the end of your next action'. Saves partial text updated to match. Consistent action-based duration language.

comments.selectedText: Sidearm
comments.comment: this could be a shotgun, not only sidearm
comments.resolution: Added shotguns to Sidearm examples: 'Pistols, shotguns, compact weapons.'

comments.selectedText: Brutal
comments.comment: what is critical with weapon die? +8, this doesn't make sense
comments.resolution: Clarified Brutal: 'damage = sum of the two 6s (12), not just the single highest (6)'. Concrete numbers, no ambiguity.

comments.selectedText: Boons self-balance: on a light weapon (1d6), the extra d6 roughly doubles your Gambit options. On a heavy weapon (2d6), it’s a modest bump. The benefit is always real but never overwhelming. Setup matters: flanking, ambush, a successful roll before combat to find an angle, planning with your team. Every Boon is another d6 in the pool, another option on the table.
comments.comment: i feel friction around lighter weapons and less gambit choices,
comments.resolution: Reframed around light weapon identity: ‘A knife (1d6) with a Boon becomes 2d6: same pool as a heavy weapon.’ Positions Boons as the light weapon path to Gambit access through setup and positioning.

comments.selectedText: Damage to an area: Roll the item’s dice against each target separately.
comments.comment: we can simplify, one roll same damage to all
comments.resolution: Simplified to ‘Roll once. Same damage to everyone in the blast.’

comments.selectedText: Single-use items with the Thrown property. Use your Act, spend the item.
comments.comment: not at close range, explosive at near could be dangerous as well
comments.resolution: Added 'Thrown range is Near. Using explosives at Close range catches you in the blast (GM sets consequence).'

comments.selectedText: Damage per target + area alight
comments.comment: area? damage to all at near range?
comments.resolution: Molotov now reads 'Damage to all at Near + area alight'. Frag reads 'Damage to all at Close of impact'. Clear blast radius.

comments.selectedText: Consumables also interact with certain tactical effects described in the Gambits section (Flash, Obscure, Deafen). If you sacrifice a die 4+ during an attack, you get the gear effect alongside damage without spending the item as a separate Act. Using the item standalone costs your Act and the item, but requires no attack roll or die sacrifice.
comments.comment: do not understand this
comments.resolution: Rewrote as two clear paths: 'sacrifice a die 4+ during an attack to trigger the effect alongside your damage, or skip your attack and use the item as your Act (no roll needed, item consumed).'

comments.selectedText: Sharp save or Snag on next action
comments.comment: how do saves or snags apply to enemies?
comments.resolution: Added clarification under 'Condition without damage': 'Enemies roll the named stat against the same result table players use (6 = resist, 4-5 = partial, 1-3 = full effect). The GM rolls for the enemy.'

comments.selectedText: Standing your ground, force of will: Nerve (thaumic effects, intimidation, psychic pressure)
comments.comment: Nerve, seeing it coming, standing in the right place, better nerve exaples here
comments.resolution: Reworded to 'Holding steady, seeing it coming, being in the right place: Nerve (thaumic effects, intimidation, standing your ground when instinct says run)'.

comments.selectedText: This is why balanced stat spreads survive encounters that specialists don’t.
comments.comment: dont shame specialists, give examples how grit and sharp could avoid this
comments.resolution: Line already removed in previous session. Thaumic example reworded to show specialists have different answers, not no answers: ‘Grit specialist might break line of sight or tackle the shaper. Sharp specialist might read the Tell and act first.’

comments.selectedText: (Defense crits don’t grant a free Gambit; that’s an attack crit reward.)
comments.comment: not needed, superflous
comments.resolution: Line already removed in previous session.

comments.selectedText: You sacrifice before reading your result.
comments.comment: what does this mean
comments.resolution: Gambits section rewritten. New text explicitly states 'You see the dice first, then decide.' No ambiguity about timing.

comments.selectedText: The 4 was below her highest die,
comments.comment: are we clear on this in the combat roll explanation?
comments.resolution: Old Rina example replaced. New Example 1 (Brian) explains the concept directly: 'The 4 was doing nothing for him anyway.' Clearer language, same point.

comments.selectedText: (1-3 per weapon
comments.comment: i think i will keep this at 1, not overloading on gambits
comments.resolution: Text already reads '1 per weapon'. The concern about overloading is addressed.

comments.selectedText: Produce greater effect: destroy cover, break a weapon, hurl someone across the room, punch through a wall
comments.comment: unarmed is 1d4, you need boon or push, is this good?
comments.resolution: Working as intended. Unarmed (1d4) can't roll a 6, so no Strong Gambits without setup. A Boon (from positioning, flanking) adds +1d6 which CAN roll 6. This is the design: unarmed fighters need tactical setup to access strong effects, matching the light weapon identity.

comments.selectedText: Threat 2 drops to 1 for this Gambit. Full effect. He goes flying.
comments.comment: your saying threat drop, but above it says full effect everywhere
comments.resolution: Rewrote example to show the progression clearly: 'Standard Gambit (4+) against Threat 2 normally lands at reduced effect: a stumble, not a real shove. But Enhanced potency (Grit 3) ignores one Threat level. The Operator resists as Threat 1 instead: full effect.'

comments.selectedText: When words are the weapon, the base Gambits take specific social forms. Same system, not separate.
comments.comment: this and the gambits below, should some be default gambits? one is a table the other are just examples, i think just hint them, how do players get them? lets see if we can consolidate this
comments.resolution: Social Gambits section removed in previous restructure. Social effects are now covered by the Influence category in the base Gambit list (Shift, Pressure, De-escalate).
